From: "Cyber Safety Team" To: "Marina Beckmann" Subject: Crypto Investment Scam Alert — Protect Your Wallet & Passive Income Date: Tue, 11 Nov 2087 07:13:00 +0100 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Cyber Safety Team — Security Alert

Dear Marina,

We are reaching out because we detected a recent rise in targeted crypto investment messages aimed at Retail Entrepreneurs in Cologne. Scammers are contacting individuals like you with offers that promise guaranteed returns and "easy" passive income, and they often ask you to transfer funds to a specific wallet or reveal access credentials.

Red flags we've observed:
  • Cold messages promising unusually high or guaranteed return on investment.
  • Pressure to act immediately or face a "lost opportunity" (creates urgency).
  • Requests to move funds to unfamiliar wallet addresses or third-party services.
  • Links with slightly misspelled domains or unusual sender addresses.

Example: a request that asks you to "secure your assets" by moving funds to a new wallet, or that guarantees passive income with no risk — these are common tactics. Do not click links or download attachments from unsolicited messages.

Learn how to spot crypto scams (Coinbase)

Report a phishing attempt (FTC) • Contact our Cyber Safety Team

What you should do now

  1. Do not reply to or click links in suspicious messages. Hover over links to confirm the real domain.
  2. Never provide private keys, seed phrases, or passwords. Legitimate services will never ask for these.
  3. If you already clicked a link or entered details, change passwords immediately and notify your wallet provider.
  4. Keep records of any communication and report the incident to local authorities and to consumer protection sites like the FTC: https://www.ftc.gov/.

We understand the appeal of new investment and trading opportunities, but please be cautious: no legitimate platform guarantees returns or promises effortless passive income with zero risk.

Stay safe,
Cyber Security & Fraud Prevention Team
Retail & Small Business Protection Unit — Cologne

Security contact: security@cybersafe.org
This message was sent to Marina (Retail Entrepreneur, Cologne). If you believe you received this in error, please contact us.
